Container.Name: owlet-bridge-native
Container.Overview:
Native Owlet Cam -> RTSP bridge, the docker-wyze-bridge way. NO emulator. Enter your Owlet account in the web control panel (http://[IP]:8088), it logs into the Owlet cloud, connects to the camera over ThroughTek/Kalay (TUTK), and serves the video as RTSP/WebRTC/HLS with a go2rtc UI (http://[IP]:1985). Point Frigate at rtsp://[IP]:18554/owlet.
EVERYTHING is configured in the UI: account credentials, region, camera DSN/UID/AuthKey, AV settings. "Connect & Diagnose" runs the real login and auto-fetches your camera key; a live log + status dashboard show exactly what's happening.
LIBRARIES: the proprietary TUTK .so libs are not shipped. Easiest path — in the web UI's "TUTK libraries" card, click "Upload & extract" and pick your Owlet .apkm/.apk; the bridge pulls the x86_64 libraries out itself (it also auto-extracts any APK left in the config folder).
NOTE on networking: this uses standard Bridge networking. Host ports are offset (1985/18554/18555) so they don't collide with Frigate's built-in go2rtc (1984/8554/8555). Keep it on Bridge (not a br0/macvlan custom network) so other containers like Frigate can reach it.

Config.Name: Control panel
Config.Description: Web UI — enter Owlet login, upload libs, see status + logs.

Config.Name: Video UI (go2rtc)
Config.Description: go2rtc UI — RTSP/WebRTC/HLS links + preview. Host 1985 to avoid Frigate's 1984.

Config.Name: RTSP
Config.Description: rtsp://[IP]:18554/owlet — point Frigate here. Host 18554 to avoid Frigate's 8554.

Config.Name: PUBLIC_RTSP_PORT
Config.Description: The HOST port you mapped RTSP (8554) to — so the UI shows reachable copy URLs. Match your RTSP port mapping above.

Config.Name: PUBLIC_HTTP_PORT
Config.Description: The HOST port you mapped the go2rtc UI (1984) to.

Config.Name: PUBLIC_WEBRTC_PORT
Config.Description: The HOST port you mapped WebRTC (8555) to.

Config.Name: WebRTC candidate (app)
Config.Description: For sub-second WebRTC to a phone/app, set this to <Unraid-LAN-IP>:<WebRTC host port>, e.g. 192.168.1.204:18555. This is the address go2rtc advertises to clients for the media connection — without it the app gets an SDP answer but no video. Leave blank if you only watch via Frigate or the LAN go2rtc UI.

Config.Name: Config (settings)
Config.Description: Persists your UI settings + camera key. Drop your Owlet .apkm here too (or upload it in the UI).

Config.Name: TUTK libs (/app/libs)
Config.Description: Folder the extracted x86_64 TUTK .so libraries live in (under x86_64/). The UI's Upload & extract writes them here.
